serves: Part 2 §10.5 / §9 (the conformance-vector honesty boundary: MLS key-distribution over the wire — the verifying-key/standing registry sourced from a real MLS Welcome, not modeled as agreed state) — earns/bounds: green-real at loopback grade — register: none — landed: RUN-08 (reproduction of the 2026-06-17 C-mls-welcome green-real run, in-environment after the Proofs fold-in).
experiment: C-mls-welcome (Workstream C, part 2) — RUN-08 in-environment reproduction
title: MLS key-distribution + standing-binding over the wire — a real openmls Welcome distributes the group key AND the lineage-folded membership/standing
reproduction_note: Byte-identical reproduction of relay-lab-runs/C-mls-welcome-2026-06-17 in the RUN-08 environment, made runnable by folding the Proofs corpus into discovery/alpha/Proofs (the lineage-mls/lineage-core path-deps now resolve). Confirms the key-distribution piece is green-real here, not only in the 2026-06-17 archive.
design_ref: Part 2 §10.5 conformance-vector footnote (the two over-the-wire pieces); F7 (proposed-changes); conformance-suite.md honesty boundary (1) 'MLS key-distribution must actually run'; open-edges.md §1 (MLS key-distribution over the wire, messaging path); the faithful crate's 'registry modeled as agreed state' honesty boundary

harness.crate: alpha/experiments/iroh/crates/mls-welcome-over-iroh (path-deps real lineage-mls/openmls 0.8.1 + iroh =1.0.0; reuses relay-loadtest's relay+endpoint code)
harness.topology: single process, real iroh + real relay (loopback): founder endpoint + joiner endpoint homed on an in-process relay; the openmls Welcome crosses a real iroh bi-stream founder->joiner
harness.group: leaves carry T1 lineage claims: alice.laptop + alice.phone under lineage-a (must fold to ONE actor, E2.9/E2.10); bob.phone under lineage-b
harness.note: the joiners' KeyPackages (public pre-keys) are handed to the founder in-process; the security-relevant KEY DISTRIBUTION + the membership/standing are what travel the wire (in the Welcome's ratchet-tree extension)

verdict: green-real: a real 1466-byte openmls Welcome travels a real iroh connection; the joiner runs join_from_welcome and derives (1) the SAME 32-byte MLS exporter secret as the founder AND (2) the SAME lineage-folded membership/standing entirely from the wire-delivered Welcome. Lands Part 1B's key-distribution piece: the verifying-key/standing registry the conformance vectors rest on is now demonstrably sourceable from a real over-the-wire MLS group, closing conformance-suite honesty boundary (1) at the spike layer.
scope_and_firewall: IN SCOPE (Part 1B): MLS key-distribution over the wire (the modeled verifying-key registry made real). Green-real, reproduced.
STILL GATED (firewall, I9): threshold-revoke as real k-of-n over the wire, and the co-sign-vs-vote authority ordering (the revocation-authority trust model). Untouched by this run.

RESIDUAL (not firewall): wiring this over-the-wire key source INTO the conformance emitter itself (today the emitter's registry is in-process). A dedicated over-the-wire vector shape would be a vector-format change (Part 1B stop rule), so it is not authored here.
open_edges: Loopback single-process (real iroh + real relay over loopback); the iroh transport itself is already proven cross-host by E10/meer/faithful, so cross-host here is a deployment detail, not new evidence.
The joiners' KeyPackages are exchanged in-process (public material); a full handshake would also carry the KeyPackage over the wire (a serialization detail, not a security one).

The standing binding here is the lineage-FOLD (membership/actor view) read from the MLS leaves; wiring a lineage-history Message's per-author signature verification to a key extracted from its MLS leaf is the next finer-grained step.
